title: Calendar Daemon Contract
description: Contract between the arkai calendar daemon and the OpenClaw agent. Same ADR-0002 pattern as email: daemon runs on host, writes JSON to workspace/input/calendar/, agent reads via safe_fs_read_text.

entrypoint.note: One-shot script, NOT a persistent daemon. Run via launchd/cron on schedule. Lives in arkai-gmail repo (shares venv + Google API deps).
entrypoint.canonical_launch_note: No API key needed (uses OAuth token). All logging to stderr. Stdout clean. Exit 0 = success, exit 1 = fatal.

GCAL_OUTPUT_DIR.description: Directory to write per-event JSON + upcoming_index.json
GCAL_OUTPUT_DIR.note: Must be readable by OpenClaw container (Docker volume mount)

GCAL_LOOKAHEAD_DAYS.description: How many days ahead to fetch events

GCAL_RETAIN_DAYS.description: Keep event files N days past event end time. Based on event end, not file mtime.
GCAL_RETAIN_DAYS.note: Set to -1 to disable retention cleanup. Only deletes files matching {12-hex}.json pattern.

GCAL_CALENDARS.description: Comma-separated calendar IDs to fetch. If empty, fetches all enabled calendars.
GCAL_CALENDARS.note: Use 'primary' for the main calendar. Example: 'primary,work@group.calendar.google.com'

GCAL_SHOW_CANCELLED.note: Set to 1 to see cancelled events (status: 'cancelled')

output_directory.file_naming: 12-char hex SHA256 of Google event ID (deterministic, idempotent)
output_directory.idempotency: Same event always produces same filename. Re-runs overwrite with latest data. Cancelled events written with status: 'cancelled'.

failure_semantics.per_event: Log error to stderr, skip failed event, continue pipeline
failure_semantics.per_calendar: Log error to stderr, skip failed calendar, continue with others
failure_semantics.fatal: Exit code 1 for auth failure or config issues
failure_semantics.success: Exit code 0 even if 0 events were fetched (empty calendar is not an error)
failure_semantics.note: Idempotent — safe to re-run. Events are overwritten, not duplicated.

retention.behavior: After each fetch, delete {12-hex}.json files where the event's end time + retain_days is in the past. Based on event end time parsed from the JSON file, not file mtime. Does not delete upcoming_index.json or non-matching files.

scheduling.concurrency: Single instance only. No locking needed — events are overwritten idempotently.
scheduling.note: Each run takes 1-3s (Google API calls only, no LLM). Much faster than email daemon.

prerequisites.google_calendar_api: Must enable Google Calendar API in the same GCP project as Gmail
prerequisites.credentials: Copy credentials.json from ~/.arkai-gmail/ to ~/.arkai-calendar/ (same OAuth client)
prerequisites.first_auth: First run will open browser for calendar.readonly consent (separate from Gmail consent)
prerequisites.python_venv: Uses ~/AI/arkai-gmail/.venv/bin/python3 — shares venv with email daemon. All deps pre-installed.

oauth_scope.note: Read-only from day one. No write operations. Token at ~/.arkai-calendar/token.json — never enters Docker container.

output_schema.description: One JSON file per calendar event. This is the exact schema of each per-event output file.

processed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this event was processed

index_schema.description: The upcoming_index.json summary file. Generated each run.

id.description: 12-char SHA256 hex of google_event_id (also the filename stem)

google_event_id.description: Original Google Calendar API event ID

status.description: Event status from Google Calendar API

start.description: Event start (ISO 8601 dateTime or YYYY-MM-DD for all-day)

end.description: Event end (ISO 8601 dateTime or YYYY-MM-DD for all-day)

all_day.description: True if this is an all-day event

html_link.description: URL to view event in Google Calendar

recurring.description: True if this is an instance of a recurring event

organizer.description: True if this attendee is the organizer

self.description: True if this attendee is the authenticated user

validation.no_api_key_needed: Calendar daemon uses only Google OAuth (calendar.readonly). No ANTHROPIC_API_KEY required. Verified: grep -c 'ANTHROPIC' scripts/calendar_daemon.py returns 0.
validation.separate_token_store: Uses ~/.arkai-calendar/token.json, separate from ~/.arkai-gmail/token.json. Different OAuth scopes prevent token reuse.
validation.retention_by_end_time: Tested: recent-end event survives, old-end event deleted, future event survives, non-matching file untouched, all-day event end date parsed correctly.
validation.output_roundtrip: CalendarDaemonResult → model_dump_json(by_alias=True) → model_validate_json() round-trip verified. Attendee 'self' field aliased correctly.
